2606.06893.title: Workflow-to-Skill: Skill Creation via Routing-Workflow-Semantics-Attachments Decomposition
2606.06893.paper_summary: Large language model agents increasingly rely on Skills to encode procedural knowledge, yet high-quality Skills remain costly to hand-write. This paper studies automatic Skill construction from heterogeneous interaction evidence, including demonstrations, agent trajectories, tool traces, and execution logs. We argue that trace-to-skill construction is not simple summarization tasks, because traces are fragmented, redundant, and may miss rare but safety-critical behaviors. To address this, we introduce RWSA, a workflow-oriented intermediate representation that decomposes Skills into Workflow structure, execution Semantics, and runtime Attachments, capturing task decomposition, control flow, verification, safety, rollback, and state management. Building on RWSA, we propose W2S, a framework that segments traces, induces local Skill drafts, aligns shared structures, reconciles branches, and compresses redundancy while preserving evidence and confidence annotations. Experiments on 70 Skills show that W2S improves behavioral replay consistency by 10.5% over summarization- and prompting-based baselines, highlighting the need to treat traces as executable runtime specifications rather than compressible text.
2606.06893.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型代理越來越依賴技能來編碼程序知識，但高品質的技能仍然需要昂貴的手動撰寫。本文研究了從異質互動證據自動構建技能的過程，包括示範、代理軌跡、工具痕跡和執行日誌。我們認為，痕跡到技能的構建並不是簡單的總結任務，因為痕跡是碎片化的、冗餘的，並且可能缺少稀有但對安全至關重要的行為。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了RWSA，一種面向工作流程的中介表示，將技能分解為工作流程結構、執行語義和運行時附件，捕捉任務分解、控制流、驗證、安全性、回滾和狀態管理。在RWSA的基礎上，我們提出了W2S，一個框架，用於分段痕跡、誘導局部技能草稿、對齊共享結構、調和分支，並在保留證據和信心註釋的同時壓縮冗餘。對70個技能的實驗表明，W2S在行為重放一致性上比基於總結和提示的基線提高了10.5%，突顯了將痕跡視為可執行運行時規範而非可壓縮文本的必要性。
2606.06893.author: Yuyang Zhang et.al.
2606.06893.authors: Yuyang Zhang, Xinyuan Han, Xudong Jiang, Run Wang
